title: Dubdi Monastery
tagline: Dubdi Monastery — spiritual town of Sikkim

overview.short: Dubdi Monastery, occasionally called Yuksom Monastery, is a Buddhist monastery of the Nyingma sect of Tibetan Buddhism near Yuksom, in the Geyzing subdivision of West Sikkim distri
overview.description: Dubdi Monastery, occasionally called Yuksom Monastery, is a Buddhist monastery of the Nyingma sect of Tibetan Buddhism near Yuksom, in the Geyzing subdivision of West Sikkim district, in Northeastern India. The Chogyar Namgyal established the first monastery known as the Dubdi Monastery in 1701, at Yuksom in Sikkim, which is part of Buddhist religious pilgrimage circuit involving the Norbugang Chorten, Pemayangtse Monastery, the Rabdentse ruins, the Sanga Choeling Monastery, the Khecheopalri Lake and the Tashiding Monastery. Established in 1701, it is professed to be the oldest monastery in Si

topPlaces.name: Yuksom
topPlaces.description: Yuksom (Nepali pronunciation: [jaksom]), also spelt Yuksum, is a historical town about 40 km north of Gyalshing in the Gyalshing district, in the northeast Indian state of Sikkim. It was the first capital of the Kingdom of Sikkim, established in 1642 by Phuntsog Namgyal, the first Chogyal of Sikkim.

topPlaces.name: Norbugang Chorten
topPlaces.description: The Norbugang Chorten (Sikkimese: ནོར་པུ་སྒང་མཆོད་རྟེན་, Wylie: nor bu sgang mchod rten) is a stupa situated in Yuksom, near the Gyalshing city of the Gyalshing district in the Indian State of Sikkim. It was erected following the crowning of the first Chogyal of Sikkim in 1642 at Narbugong Coronation Throne near Yuksom (Gyalshing).

topPlaces.name: Khecheopalri Lake
topPlaces.description: Khecheopalri Lake, originally known as Kha-Chot-Palri (meaning the heaven of Padmasambhava), is a lake located near Khecheopalri village, 147 kilometres (91 mi) west of Gangtok in the West Sikkim district of the Northeastern Indian state of Sikkim. Located 34 kilometres (21 mi) to the northwest of Pelling town, the lake is sacred for both Buddhists and Hindus, and is believed to be a wish fulfilling lake.

topPlaces.name: Melli Atsing Monastery
topPlaces.description: Melli Atsing Monastery (a.k.a. Mallu Monastery) is a Buddhist monastery in Sikkim, northeastern India.

topPlaces.name: Pemayangtse Monastery
topPlaces.description: The Pemayangtse Monastery is a Buddhist monastery in Pemayangtse, near Gyalshing city in Gyalshing district in the northeastern Indian state of Sikkim, located 6 km from Gyalshing city, the district headquarters, 110 km west of Gangtok. Planned, designed and founded by Lama Lhatsun Chempo in 1647, it is one of the oldest and premier monasteries of Sikkim, also the most famous in Sikkim.

topPlaces.name: Rabdentse
topPlaces.description: Rabdentse was the second capital of the former Kingdom of Sikkim from 1670 to 1814. The capital city was destroyed by the invading Gurkha army and only the ruins of the palace and the chortens are seen here now.

topPlaces.name: Chenrezig statue and skywalk
topPlaces.description: The Chenrezig statue and skywalk are a tourist attraction in Pelling, Gyalshing district, Sikkim. The complex houses a 137 ft (42 m) tall statue of the bodhisattva Chenrezig, the tallest Chenrezig statue in the world.

topPlaces.name: Darap
topPlaces.description: Darap is a census village in Gyalshing district, Sikkim, India. As per the 2011 Census of India, Darap village has a total population of 1,743 people including 901 males and 842 females.

items.note: Yuksom (Nepali pronunciation: [jaksom]), also spelt Yuksum, is a historical town about 40 km north of Gyalshing in the Gyalshing district, in the northeast Indian state of Sikkim. It was the first capital of the Kingdom of Sikkim, established in 1642 by Phuntsog Namgyal, the first Chogyal of Sikkim.

The Norbugang Chorten (Sikkimese: ནོར་པུ་སྒང་མཆོད་རྟེན་, Wylie: nor bu sgang mchod rten) is a stupa situated in Yuksom, near the Gyalshing city of the Gyalshing district in the Indian State of Sikkim. It was erected following the crowning of the first Chogyal of Sikkim in 1642 at Narbugong Coronation Throne near Yuksom (Gyalshing).

Khecheopalri Lake, originally known as Kha-Chot-Palri (meaning the heaven of Padmasambhava), is a lake located near Khecheopalri village, 147 kilometres (91 mi) west of Gangtok in the West Sikkim district of the Northeastern Indian state of Sikkim. Located 34 kilometres (21 mi) to the northwest of Pelling town, the lake is sacred for both Buddhists and Hindus, and is believed to be a wish fulfilling lake.
Melli Atsing Monastery (a.k.a. Mallu Monastery) is a Buddhist monastery in Sikkim, northeastern India.

The Pemayangtse Monastery is a Buddhist monastery in Pemayangtse, near Gyalshing city in Gyalshing district in the northeastern Indian state of Sikkim, located 6 km from Gyalshing city, the district headquarters, 110 km west of Gangtok. Planned, designed and founded by Lama Lhatsun Chempo in 1647, it is one of the oldest and premier monasteries of Sikkim, also the most famous in Sikkim.

Rabdentse was the second capital of the former Kingdom of Sikkim from 1670 to 1814. The capital city was destroyed by the invading Gurkha army and only the ruins of the palace and the chortens are seen here now.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — Chenrezig statue and skywalk & around

items.note: The Chenrezig statue and skywalk are a tourist attraction in Pelling, Gyalshing district, Sikkim. The complex houses a 137 ft (42 m) tall statue of the bodhisattva Chenrezig, the tallest Chenrezig statue in the world.
Darap is a census village in Gyalshing district, Sikkim, India. As per the 2011 Census of India, Darap village has a total population of 1,743 people including 901 males and 842 females.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dubdi Monastery?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dubdi Monastery is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dubdi Monastery?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pakyong Airport, Gangtok (39 km) and the nearest railway station is Siliguri Junction (75 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dubdi Monastery?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dubdi Monastery at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dubdi Monastery?
faq.a: Stays in Dubdi Monastery start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Dubdi Monastery?
faq.a: The top attractions are Yuksom, Norbugang Chorten, Khecheopalri Lake, Melli Atsing Monastery, Pemayangtse Monastery.
